channel.title: Projects on Siddhant Maharana
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Siddhant Maharana

item.title: Counting Objects using OpenCV and RCNN models
item.description: Problem Statement Count the number of objects in the image. Approach Used OpenCV libraries involving thresholding, edge detection, contour detection to count the objects. Used RCNN and SSD models trained on COCO dataset using Tensorflow Object Detection API Results OpenCV performed great when there were non overlapping images. RCNN models outperformed OpenCV results and SSD trained networks on images with objects pretrained using COCO dataset. Link to Code

item.title: Designing a Classifier and comparing word emdeddings
item.description: Problem Statement Train a sentiment classifier based on the text of Forms 10-K,8-K. Compare the embedding layers trained in the classifier with another embedding layers trained using word2vec approach. Approach Extracted text from the SEC documents to form the embedding layer. Trained a sentiment classifer model using LSTM and RNN networks and adjusted the parameters on the basis of the labels derived from the stock market price movements.

item.title: Predicting Trip Duration using PySpark
item.description: Problem Statement Predict trip duration for the New York Taxis using historical data worth 10GB using PySpark modules. Approach Ingested data using Spark’s RDD and dataframed to perform analyses and extract features. Used Spark ML libraries to perform regression and clustering on the data and predict trip duration. Results Features including traffic clusters, hot spot locations in addition to the existing features improved the prediction power of the regression model.

item.title: Predicting crimes using spatio temporal and demographics data
item.description: Problem Statement Predict the occurence of the type of crime using historical crime data. Approach Designed features using historical and other data related to demographics and time. Used several machine learning algorithms to build a multi class classifier. Results XGBoost tuned optimally, performed the best among all other classifiers. Link to Code

item.title: Sentiment classifier using Twitter data
item.description: Problem Statement Design a classifier that can detect sentiments in the tweets. Approach Vectorized the text data using bag-of-words technique and also engineered features based on other parameters from the tweets. Modelled and tuned probabilistic and other models to perform the classification. Results Finely tuned Random forest perfomed best among Naive Baeyes, SVC and other classifiers. Link to Code

item.title: Undestanding factors that affect FINRA arbitration decisions
item.description: Problem Statement Analyse the FINRA documents to find the factors that affect the decisions made by the judge. Approach Extracted documents from FINRA website to gather corpus of text documents and metadata available at the website and extracted features relevant to the analysis. Performed regression modeling and statistical analysis to determine how various factors affect the decisions. Results Factors such as the type and number of representation, regions of the hearing sites, types of claims filed had impact on the outcome of the arbitration decisions.
